Why do you think it spreads like that.

story.description: A clip of cats / kittens: elegant black cat strolling in a garden
story.hook: Cats show why the grooming signal matters.
story.lead: Black cats aren’t just sleek—their fur isn’t just dark. Look at how light catches those tiny copper flecks along the spine. That’s not dirt. Those are guard hairs catching sunlight differently because their cores are hollow. That hollow core scatters light at odd angles, breaking up their outline so fast-moving prey can’t lock onto them. That’s why ambush predators like this one vanish in plain si
story.script: Black cats aren’t just sleek—their fur isn’t just dark. Look at how light catches those tiny copper flecks along the spine. That’s not dirt. Those are guard hairs catching sunlight differently because their cores are hollow. That hollow core scatters light at odd angles, breaking up their outline so fast-moving prey can’t lock onto them. That’s why ambush predators like this one vanish in plain sight. Ever notice flecks on your own cat’s fur? Spot one in the light and you’ll see the trick.
